feed.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes

entry.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes
entry.summary:
The discussion revolves around the recent meeting on Taproot activation, the details of which can be found in the meeting log available at [http://gnusha.org/taproot-activation/2021-03-23.log](http://gnusha.org/taproot-activation/2021-03-23.log), and was also livestreamed by Jeremy on his Twitch channel, accessible through [https://www.twitch.tv/videos/960346848](https://www.twitch.tv/videos/960346848). The primary focus of contention is the method of activation for Taproot, particularly the debate over using block heights exclusively or a combination of block heights and Median Time Past (MTP) within the same activation mechanism. The preference for a consistent use of block height is documented in a detailed comment on GitHub ([https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21377issuecomment-802336038](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/21377issuecomment-802336038)), advocating against the mix due to its complexity and potential confusion.
Two main arguments are presented in favor of employing a mixed approach of block heights and MTP: the need for additional review to ensure a height-based activation's effectiveness and the desire to circumvent a market-driven push for launching a User-Activated Soft Fork (UASF) client. However, the argument against these points emphasizes that the necessity for further review does not pose an excessive burden, especially if efforts are focused on reviewing proposals that maintain consistency in activation mechanisms. Additionally, it is suggested that making technical decisions based on speculative marketing strategies diverges from Bitcoin Core's foundational principles.
The argument strongly favors using block heights for their simplicity in understanding timing and intervals during different phases of the activation process, as opposed to the complications introduced by mixing block heights with MTP. This stance is backed by a belief in the importance of maintaining clarity and consistency in technical decisions, avoiding unnecessary complexities that could hinder the overall process and understanding of Taproot's activation mechanism.
